mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines by > 0.25pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.3% signals tightening labor markets that drive corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 20d returns in > 50% of cases where UNRATE declines by > 0.3pp over 3 months, or t-test p > 0.1 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong demand pressures that elevate corporate margins, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of periods where INDPRO and CPIAUCSL both rise by > 1.5% YoY, or interaction term coefficient < 0.1 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A housing market boom (HOUST > 1487k units) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS > 738.366B) signals strong private demand that drives S&P 500 gains during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 30% of instances where HOUST and RSAFS both exceed their 3-month moving averages, or regime-specific IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 330.293 (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank hawkishness that raises real rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5d returns in > 50% of cases where CPIAUCSL exceeds 330.293, or t-test p > 0.05 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 158,637k) indicates robust employment that supports consumer spending, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS rises by > 100k, or Rank IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals expanding private sector demand that drives S&P 500 gains during recovery phases.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 35% of periods where GDPC1 and HOUST both rise by > 1% QoQ, or interaction term coefficient < 0.15 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 102.551 signals improving supply chain efficiency that reduces inflationary pressures and boosts equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 5d returns in > 50% of cases where INDPRO rises by > 1.5% MoM, or t-test p > 0.05 for mean return difference.
